vBRIEFInfo.description: Scope vBRIEF ingested from GitHub issue #1555

plan.title: tooling: make GitHub Markdown body posting safe from shell substitution

narratives.Description: Add a canonical safe path for posting Markdown-rich GitHub issue, comment, and PR bodies so agents do not accidentally let the shell interpret backticks or other metacharacters. The story covers a reusable helper or task surface, documentation, and tests that preserve literal Markdown through GitHub mutations.
narratives.ImplementationPlan: Implement a small GitHub body-posting helper or task wrapper that accepts stdin or a file and invokes `gh api` through safe subprocess handling without shell interpolation. Update SCM guidance and agent prompt guidance with examples for create, edit, and live read-back, then add tests with backticks, dollar signs, quotes, and fenced code blocks.
narratives.UserStory: As a directive agent operator, I want GitHub Markdown body posting to avoid shell interpolation, so that issue comments and PR bodies preserve the exact text I intended.
narratives.Overview:
## Summary
Agents can corrupt GitHub issue/comment bodies when Markdown containing backticks is embedded inside double-quoted shell commands. In Bash, text like `` `ghx` `` is command substitution, so the shell executes the command and replaces the intended literal Markdown before `gh api` receives the payload.

This happened during WSL validation while adding a `ghx` requirement to #1554: the intended phrase "include `ghx`" was posted as "include Work seamlessly with GitHub from the command line" because the shell expanded the backticked `ghx` token.

## Problem
Directive currently tells agents to prefer task/gh surfaces, but it does not give a deterministic safe path for posting Markdown-rich issue comments, PR bodies, or issue bodies. This leaves agents to hand-roll shell quoting around Markdown, which is brittle for backticks, dollar signs, here-doc boundaries, and other shell metacharacters.

## Proposal
Add a canonical safe body-posting surface and guidance, for example:

- A task wrapper that accepts body text from stdin or a file and calls `gh api` with `shell=False` / safe subprocess handling.
- Documentation/rules that forbid inline Markdown bodies with backticks inside double-quoted shell commands.
- Examples for creating/editing GitHub issues and comments using JSON/stdin, single-quoted heredocs, or temporary body files.
- Read-back guidance: use live `gh` immediately after mutations, because `ghx` may return cached stale GET responses.

## Acceptance criteria
- Directive rules or SCM docs explicitly warn that Markdown backticks inside double-quoted shell commands trigger shell command substitution.
- A canonical helper/task exists for posting GitHub issue/comment/PR Markdown bodies without shell interpolation.
- The helper uses safe subprocess capture (`encoding="utf-8", errors="replace"`) and avoids `shell=True`.
- Examples cover issue creation, issue comment creation, and issue comment edit/read-back.
- Tests cover a body containing literal backticks, dollar signs, quotes, and a fenced code block.

Refs #1554.

items.title: Add safe GitHub body posting helper

narrative.Acceptance: Given a Markdown body containing literal backticks, when the canonical helper posts it to a GitHub issue comment, then the live read-back contains the same backtick text unchanged.

items.title: Document shell-substitution hazards and safe examples

narrative.Acceptance: Given the SCM and agent guidance, when an agent needs to post GitHub Markdown, then the docs show safe create, edit, and read-back examples that do not embed Markdown in double-quoted shell commands.

items.title: Use safe subprocess and live read-back semantics

narrative.Acceptance: Given the helper implementation, when tests inspect the subprocess invocation, then it avoids `shell=True`, uses UTF-8 replacement-safe capture, and performs mutation read-back through live `gh`.

items.title: Cover literal Markdown payloads in tests

narrative.Acceptance: Given a test payload with backticks, dollar signs, quotes, and a fenced code block, when the helper builds the GitHub API request, then the serialized body preserves each literal character.

swarm.missing_traces_justification: Issue #1555 was created from the observed WSL GitHub comment mutation failure and references #1554 as the triggering incident.

references.title: Issue #1555: tooling: make GitHub Markdown body posting safe from shell substitution
